item.title: Baidu’s AI Chip Unit Plans Dual IPO in Shanghai and Hong Kong
item.description: Baidu Inc.’s chip unit Kunlunxin is planning an initial public offering on Shanghai’s Nasdaq-style bourse in addition to a separate listing plan in Hong Kong as the Chinese search engine giant looks to taps investor appetite for semiconductor stocks.

item.title: Goldman-Backed Go Taxi Seeks $1.3 Billion Valuation in Tokyo IPO
item.description: Taxi-hailing app operator Go Inc. is targeting a valuation of about ¥200 billion ($1.3 billion) for its initial public offering that may raise as much as ¥90 billion, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: Philippines Downplays Stagflation Risk, Vows to Boost Spending
item.description: The Philippines pledged a rapid ramp-up in government spending starting this month to revive an economy hit by a sharp slowdown in growth and surging consumer prices.

item.title: Russians Weary of War and Repression Start to Blame the Kremlin
item.description: For years, many Russians cheered on Vladimir Putin’s full-scale invasion of Ukraine in a wave of nationalist fervor. Others kept silent, fearful of the state cracking down against even mild criticism of the social and economic fallout.

item.title: Europe’s Power Network Is Getting a Wartime Wakeup Call
item.description: Grid operators are building new defenses against sabotage and cyberattacks

item.title: Switzerland’s Population Cap Idea Splits Voters Down the Middle
item.description: Switzerland looks set for a nail-biter vote on a proposal to cap its population at 10 million people, with a new poll showing the public is split down the middle on the issue.

item.title: Indonesia Foreign Reserves Fall Further to Two-Year Low in April
item.description: Indonesia’s foreign-exchange reserves fell further in April to the lowest in nearly two years as the central bank stepped up intervention to defend the rupiah.

item.title: Markets Debate If Japan Sold Treasuries When Intervening in Yen
item.description: The Federal Reserve’s custody holdings of Treasuries fell for the first time in a month at a time Japan was likely intervening to support its currency, with markets debating whether the nation offloaded US securities to fund its yen purchases.

item.title: Sony Announces $3 Billion Buyback as Memory Prices Take Toll
item.description: Sony Group Corp. will buy back up to ¥500 billion ($3.2 billion) of its shares, the company said as it set out a profit forecast largely in line with expectations on Friday.

item.title: Australia’s Battery Boom Starts to Crowd Lucrative Power Trade
item.description: Australia’s big-battery build-out is starting to crowd the lucrative trade that made the assets attractive: buying up power when prices are low and selling it back when they spike, capturing the difference.

item.title: Adani Energy Stocks Emerge as Proxy for India’s AI Trade
item.description: Group’s push into green-powered data centers is driving gains in its energy units.

item.title: Tencent, Alibaba Face Slowing Growth as AI Costs Mount
item.description: Tencent Holdings Ltd. and Alibaba Group Holding Ltd. earnings will be shaped by rising artificial intelligence investment costs and growing competition in China’s AI sector, following DeepSeek’s high-profile V4 model launch.

item.title: Malaysia to Seek Norway Explanation Over Missile Supply Halt
item.description: Malaysia plans to seek clarification from Norway over a halt in the supply of naval strike missiles following a change in defense export policies — a further setback for a long-delayed combat ship project.

item.title: Citi Targets Hedge Funds, Private Equity in FX Growth Push
item.description: Citigroup Inc. is expanding its foreign-exchange business with hedge fund and private-equity clients, part of a broader push as global trading volumes rise.

item.title: South Korea’s Current Account Surplus Surges to Record on Chips
item.description: South Korea’s current-account surplus surged to another record in March, driven by a surge in semiconductor exports and stronger shipments across Asia and the US, even as foreign investors sharply reduced holdings of domestic stocks amid rising Middle East tensions.

item.title: Billionaire Piramal’s Shadow Bank Plans $1 Billion Foreign Debt
item.description: Billionaire Ajay Piramal’s Indian shadow lender is looking to raise as much as $1 billion in foreign currency loans this year to meet strong demand for retail credit in the world’s fastest-growing major economy.

item.title: No AI, Poor Returns Drive Indian Investors to Foreign Markets
item.description: Investors in India, long focused almost entirely on domestic markets, are increasingly starting to look outward.

item.title: Principal Eyes $3 Billion for Two Data Center Funds on AI Boom
item.description: Principal Financial Group Inc. is seeking to raise $3 billion for two funds this year to capitalize on demand for investment in data centers in the US and Europe, according to people familiar with the matter.

item.title: AI Trade Turns Adani’s Energy Stocks Into India Proxy Play
item.description: Adani Group’s push into green-powered data centers is driving gains in its energy units, which have become one of the few artificial intelligence trades in India.

item.title: Gundlach Takes Longshot Bet in Case of US Debt Restructuring
item.description: DoubleLine Capital’s Jeffrey Gundlach is repositioning some of his funds for the extreme scenario that the US government could choose to restructure its debt in response to a potential future recession.

item.title: Trump Rejects Need for Curbs on Oil Exports After Trade Surge
item.description: President Donald Trump said there’s no need for curbs on US oil or jet fuel exports to address global shortages sparked by the war with Iran.

item.title: Nvidia CEO Says He Would Join Trump’s China Trip If Invited
item.description: Nvidia Corp. Chief Executive Officer Jensen Huang said he would gladly join President Donald Trump on an upcoming visit to China if invited, signaling that the leader of America’s most valuable company has yet to receive an offer to go.

item.title: Australia Moves to Toughen Pension Funds’ Performance Scorecard
item.description: The Australian government is moving to tighten an annual performance test for the nation’s A$4.5 trillion ($3.2 trillion) pension industry as concerns grow the current model may be steering investment away from some sectors.

item.title: Australia to ‘Try to Thread the Needle’ in Budget Tested by War
item.description: Australian Treasurer Jim Chalmers aims to deliver a budget that tackles inflation, revives productivity and reins in deficits, a tough challenge at a time when fuel prices are soaring because of the war in the Middle East.

item.title: Singapore Tests Two for Hantavirus After Deadly Cruise Outbreak
item.description: Singapore has isolated two residents who were onboard a cruise ship linked to a deadly outbreak of hantavirus.

item.title: Japan’s Real Wages Rise For Third Month to Keep BOJ on Hike Path
item.description: Japanese workers’ real wages rose in March for a third consecutive month, supporting the Bank of Japan’s case for further interest rate hikes even as the Middle East conflict clouds the economic outlook.

item.title: Foreign Central Banks Boost Share of Malaysian Bonds to Record
item.description: Foreign central banks and governments have raised their share of Malaysian sovereign bonds to a record, underscoring the nation’s growing appeal as a reserve asset in a volatile global environment.

item.title: Asia Heat Waves Spell Double Trouble for Economies Hit by Oil
item.description: Asia is grappling with a new threat to inflation on top of an oil shock as a looming El Niño brings soaring temperatures and dry weather to countries from India to New Zealand, driving up food costs.

item.title: MP Sees Heavy Rare Earth Demand Falling as Technology Advances
item.description: MP Materials Corp. expects demand for some of the most expensive rare earth materials to drop sharply as magnet makers adopt alternative metals.

item.title: Goldman Sachs Private Credit Fund’s Share of Bad Loans Rises
item.description: A publicly-traded private credit fund managed by Goldman Sachs Group Inc. put two additional companies on non-accrual status in the first quarter, as the industry grapples with mounting concerns over exposure to businesses vulnerable to AI-driven disruption.
